title: Can't set output of combinatorial logic
body:
Verilator command:
`verilator --cc --exe --build -j 0 --public-flat-rw --vpi --Wno-fatal --trace --hierarchical sim_main.cpp top.sv`

Version:
Verilator 5.036 2025-04-27 rev v5.036

OS:
AlmaLinux 8.10

I'm trying to set the output of combinatorial logic. `counter_d` in this instance.

```
always_comb begin
    counter_d = counter_q;
    if (clear_i) begin
        counter_d = '0;
    end else if (load_i) begin
        counter_d = {1'b0, d_i};
    end else if (en_i) begin
        if (down_i) begin
            counter_d = counter_q - delta_i;
        end else begin
            counter_d = counter_q + delta_i;
        end
    end
end

always_ff @(posedge clk_i or negedge rst_ni) begin
    if (!rst_ni) begin
       counter_q <= '0;
    end else begin
       counter_q <= counter_d;
    end
end
```

In C++
```
top->rootp->top__DOT__dut__DOT__i_counter__DOT__counter_d = 1;
```

I would expect `counter_d` to become `1` until `top->eval()` is called and `counter_q` to become `1` after `top->eval()`. In reality, absolutely nothing changes when I add that C++ line.

If I force the signal then it works as expected.

Is this intended behavior?
